title: श्री ओंकारेश्वर स्तोत्रम्
devanagariText:
॥ श्री ओंकारेश्वर स्तोत्रम् ॥

ॐ नमः शिवाय ओंकारेश्वराय।
नर्मदातीरवासिने महेश्वराय॥

ओंकाररूपमजमव्ययमीश्वरं तं
नर्मदया परिवृतं शिवमोंकृतीशम्।
मान्धातृपूजितपदं जगदेकनाथं
ओंकारमीश्वरमहं प्रणमामि नित्यम्॥ १॥

द्वीपे शिवाकृतिसमे नरनाथसेव्ये
नर्मोदकल्लोलपरिप्लुतमण्डलस्थम्।
ज्योतिर्मयं गिरिवरे विमले विभान्तं
ओंकारमीश्वरमहं प्रणमामि शम्भुम्॥ २॥

ओंकारबीजमखिलस्य जगत्प्रसूतिं
आद्यक्षरं परमशिल्पिविनिर्मिताङ्गम्।
प्रत्यक्षमीश्वरपदं गगनोपमानं
ओंकारमीश्वरमहं प्रणमामि देवम्॥ ३॥

मान्धाता नृपतिर्धीमान् यत्र तेपे तपो महत्।
शिवं प्रसाद्य वरदं प्राप लोकत्रयं यशः॥ ४॥

विन्ध्यपर्वतसानूनां मध्ये नर्मदयोर्जले।
ओंकारलिङ्गमुद्भूतं चतुर्थं ज्योतिरात्मकम्॥ ५॥

नर्मदायास्तटे पुण्ये मङ्गलद्वीपमध्यगम्।
ओंकारलिङ्गमासाद्य मुच्यते सर्वपातकैः॥ ६॥

प्रणवस्वरूपं परमेश्वरं तं
त्र्यम्बकं चन्द्रकलावतंसम्।
भस्मोद्धूलितसर्वाङ्गं
ओंकारेश्वरमाश्रये॥ ७॥

विन्ध्यपृष्ठे महापुण्ये सिद्धक्षेत्रे सुशोभने।
स्नात्वा नर्मदया साक्षात् पूजयित्वा महेश्वरम्॥ ८॥

ओंकारेशं शिवं शान्तं शाश्वतं वरदं हरम्।
नमामि शिरसा भक्त्या लिङ्गरूपं सदाशिवम्॥ ९॥

ओंकारेश्वरमाहात्म्यं यः पठेच्छृणुयादपि।
सर्वपापविनिर्मुक्तः शिवसायुज्यमाप्नुयात्॥ १०॥

॥ इति श्री ओंकारेश्वर स्तोत्रम् सम्पूर्णम् ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Omkareshwar Stotram ||

Om Namah Shivaya Omkareshwaraya |
Narmada-tira-vasine Maheshwaraya ||

Omkara-rupam-ajam-avyayam-Ishwaram tam
Narmadaya parivritam Shivam-Omkritisham |
Mandhatra-pujita-padam jagad-eka-natham
Omkaram-Ishwaram-aham pranamami nityam || 1 ||

Dvipe Shiva-akriti-same nara-natha-sevye
Narmodaka-allola-paripluta-mandala-stham |
Jyotirmayam girivare vimale vibhantam
Omkaram-Ishwaram-aham pranamami Shambhum || 2 ||

Omkara-bijam-akhilasya jagat-prasutim
Adya-aksharam parama-shilpi-vinirmitangam |
Pratyaksham-Ishwara-padam gaganopamanam
Omkaram-Ishwaram-aham pranamami Devam || 3 ||

Mandhata nripatir-dhiman yatra tepe tapo mahat |
Shivam prasadya varadam prapa loka-trayam yashah || 4 ||

Vindhya-parvata-sanoonam madhye Narmadayor-jale |
Omkara-lingam-udbhutam chaturtham jyotir-atmakam || 5 ||

Narmadayas-tate punye Mangala-dvipa-madhyagam |
Omkara-lingam-asadya muchyate sarva-patakaih || 6 ||

Pranava-svarupam Parameshwaram tam
Tryambakam Chandrakala-avatamsam |
Bhasmoddulita-sarvangam
Omkareshwaram-ashraye || 7 ||

Vindhya-prishtthe maha-punye Siddha-kshetre sushobhane |
Snatva Narmadaya sakshat pujayitva Maheshwaram || 8 ||

Omkaresham Shivam Shantam shashvatam varadam Haram |
Namami shirasa bhaktya linga-rupam Sadashivam || 9 ||

Omkareshwara-mahatmyam yah pathech-chrinuyad-api |
Sarva-papa-vinirmuktah Shiva-sayujyam-apnuyat || 10 ||

|| Iti Shri Omkareshwar Stotram Sampurnam ||
hindiMeaning: ओंकार रूप, अज, अव्यय, नर्मदा से परिवृत, मान्धाता द्वारा पूजित, जगदेकनाथ ओंकारेश्वर को नित्य प्रणाम। शिवाकृति द्वीप पर नर्मदा के जल से परिप्लुत मण्डल में ज्योतिर्मय विभान्त शम्भु को नमन। ओंकार बीज, अखिल जगत की प्रसूति, आद्याक्षर, परमशिल्पी निर्मित, प्रत्यक्ष ईश्वर पद, गगन उपमान देव को प्रणाम। विन्ध्य पर्वत के मध्य नर्मदा जल में उद्भूत यह चतुर्थ ज्योतिर्लिंग है। इसके दर्शन मात्र से सर्व पातक नष्ट होते हैं और शिव सायुज्य प्राप्त होता है।
benefits: Equivalent to pilgrimage to Omkareshwar, the fourth Jyotirlinga
Traditionally recited for removal of all sins by mere darshan of the lingam
Invokes the merit of chanting pranava (om) perpetually
Invokes the blessing of shiva-sayujya (union with shiva) after death
Hearing or reading this grants liberation
Classical tradition invokes this for removal of obstacles and bestows divine grace
description: The Omkareshwar Stotram, known in Sanskrit as श्री ओंकारेश्वर स्तोत्रम्, is a devotional hymn of praise in praise of Lord Shiva. This sacred composition is sourced from Shiva Purana and continues to inspire devotees worldwide. Devotees recite this prayer to bestow Shiva-sayujya (union with Shiva) after death and equivalent to pilgrimage to Omkareshwar, the fourth Jyotirlinga. It is also said to destroy all sins by mere darshan of the lingam. It is especially recommended on Monday (Somvar) and during Maha Shivaratri and Shravan Somvar for maximum spiritual benefit. Comprising 10 verses, it can be completed in approximately 8 minutes, making it suitable for daily devotional practice. On this page, you can read the complete Omkareshwar Stotram in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Read Omkareshwar Stotram in Sanskrit with transliteration and Hindi meaning. Sacred hymn to the fourth Jyotirlinga on Narmada river island.

seoFaqs.question: What does the name Omkareshwar mean?
seoFaqs.answer: Omkareshwar literally means 'the Lord of Omkara,' the cosmic syllable Om. Shiva is venerated here as the embodiment of the primordial pranava sound from which all creation arose. The shrine sits on Mandhata island in the Narmada River in Madhya Pradesh's Khandwa district, an island whose natural shape resembles the Devanagari letter ॐ when viewed from above. The stotra invokes Shiva as the formless source of sound that takes audible form for the sake of devotees. (Source: Wikipedia: Omkareshwar Temple; Simhastha guide; Tirth.com.)

seoFaqs.question: Where is Omkareshwar mentioned in scripture?
seoFaqs.answer: Omkareshwar is named in the Dwadasa Jyotirlinga Stotram as one of the twelve self-manifested jyotirlingas of Lord Shiva. Its sthala-mahatmya (legend of place) is detailed in the Skanda Purana, the Shiva Purana, and the Vayu Purana. The Skanda Purana narrates how the Vindhya mountain performed severe austerities to Shiva to gain supremacy over the Himalayas, and Shiva manifested as a jyotirlinga to bless him. This is among the earliest and most authoritative scriptural attestations of the shrine. (Source: Skanda Purana; Shiva Purana; Hindupedia Dwadasa Jyothir Linga Stotram.)

seoFaqs.question: Who was King Mandhata and how is he linked to this stotra?
seoFaqs.answer: King Mandhata, an ancestor of Lord Rama in the Ikshvaku (Suryavansha) lineage, performed intense tapas on this very island until Shiva manifested as Omkareshwara to grant him darshan. The island and the surrounding mountain are named Mandhata in his honour. The stotra often references this royal devotee as proof that even rulers and emperors must approach Shiva with humility, and that the jyotirlinga rewards sincere penance regardless of station. (Source: Skanda Purana; Behind Every Temple; Bhakti Bharat.)

seoFaqs.question: Why does Omkareshwar have two lingas, Omkareshwar and Mamaleshwar?
seoFaqs.answer: Unique among the twelve jyotirlingas, Omkareshwar features two principal Shiva lingas. The Omkareshwara linga sits on the Mandhata island, while the Mamaleshwar (also spelt Amareshwar) linga stands on the southern bank of the Narmada. Tradition holds that when Shiva manifested, he split into two so that both groups of devotees, those on the island and those on the mainland, could receive equal darshan. Pilgrims complete a parikrama covering both shrines along with the sacred Narmada. (Source: Wikipedia: Omkareshwar Temple; Pravase; Bhakti Bharat.)

seoFaqs.question: What benefits does the Omkareshwar Stotram offer?
seoFaqs.answer: Traditional Shaiva sources state that reciting the Omkareshwar Stotram and meditating on this jyotirlinga removes deep-rooted karmic patterns, grants the spiritual fruit of completing all twelve jyotirlinga darshans, and bestows steadiness in pranava (Om) meditation. Pilgrims chant the stotra during the Narmada parikrama, during annakuta offerings, and during Maha Shivaratri at the temple. The shrine is also associated with ancestral peace, since the Narmada banks are a recognised site for shraadh and pinda dana ceremonies. (Source: Devshoppe; Vedic Folks; Tirth.com.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the best time to visit and chant at Omkareshwar?
seoFaqs.answer: The temple holds special significance during the entire Shravan month, on every Pradosha, on Maha Shivaratri, and on Karthik Purnima when the Narmada banks come alive with deepdaan. The shayana aarti at night, when Shiva and Parvati are said to play chausar (dice) in the sanctum, is among the most distinctive rituals at any jyotirlinga. Visitors typically chant the stotra during morning bhasma aarti, after a Narmada snan, before entering the sanctum for darshan. (Source: Pravase; Bhakti Bharat; Simhastha guide.)
